source_alignment.subject: Science
source_alignment.source_statement: Identify and name a variety of common wild and garden plants, including deciduous and evergreen trees.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches plant identification through audio-first observation, sorting and garden exploration.

objective.child_goal: I can spot and name common plants and trees.
objective.statement: Identify common wild plants, garden plants, deciduous trees and evergreen trees using simple observable features.
misconceptions: Calls every plant a flower even when it is a tree or grass.
Thinks evergreen means the tree is always growing flowers.
Sorts by favourite colour rather than observable plant features.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A plant that grows without someone planting it in a garden.
vocabulary.audio_script: A wild plant grows by itself in places like parks or paths.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A dandelion growing beside a path.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A tree that keeps green leaves or needles through the year.
vocabulary.audio_script: Evergreen trees stay green through the year.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A pine tree still green in winter.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Tap the plant part you can see.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Scientists look closely before they name things.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Large plant pictures with leaf, stem and flower hotspots.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Open the garden scanner and find three kinds of plant.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce plants as a varied group, not just flowers.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Some plants are flowers, some are trees, and some are grasses.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A child-safe garden scanner sweeps over plants.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: This pine is an evergreen tree because it stays green.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model simple classification using visible features.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A pine tree keeps green needles, so it is evergreen.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Sort the pictures into wild plants, garden plants and trees.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Drag each picture to the group it belongs to.
Evergreen trees keep their green leaves or needles.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Pip why a tree is also a plant.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I thought plants were only flowers. Can you show me?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion compares flower, grass and tree cards.

manipulatives.purpose: Let young learners observe and sort plant images with audio support.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners tap hotspots, hear plant names, sort cards and compare seasonal tree views.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Audio-first prompts, large image cards, no reading requirement and reduced-motion static seasons.

manipulatives.purpose: Build identification from observable clues such as leaf shape, flowers, bark and whether a tree keeps its leaves.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners move a pretend magnifying lens between large hotspots, reveal one clue at a time, then tap the matching plant badge; the clue trail rewards careful looking rather than speed.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Hotspots can be reached by keyboard or single-switch scanning and selected without dragging; static mode presents the same labelled close-ups in a simple panel, with reduced-motion fades disabled.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave naming and sorting only after children can observe features with support.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Name common plants from picture and spoken clues.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: plant photo/illustration + spoken clue + three picture choices
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use UK-familiar examples and clear non-stock-like imagery.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Sort common plants into broad groups using observable features.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: plant card + group baskets + audio labels
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid ambiguous cultivated/wild examples at first.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Recognise evergreen trees by leaves/needles staying green.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: season panels + tree choices + tap evergreen
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Explain deciduous without requiring the term as mastery.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain common plant names and feature language through warm-ups.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue + picture clue + spoken name
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep repetition calm and predictable for young learners.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use simple observation language to justify a plant sort.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: plant picture + feature hotspots + because prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Accept oral explanation in early-year mode.

hints: A tree is a kind of plant.
question_variants.explanation: An oak tree is a tree, and a tree is a plant.

hints: Look for green needles or leaves.
question_variants.explanation: The pine is evergreen because it stays green through the year.

body.prompt: Sort the dandelion into the best basket.

question_variants.explanation: A dandelion is a common wild plant.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Calls every plant a flower even when it is a tree or grass.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner sorts trees or grasses as flowers.
repair_sequence: Show flower, grass and tree as all plants.
Sort a new mixed set together.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner identifies a tree or grass as a plant in a new item.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks evergreen means the tree is always growing flowers.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses a flowering tree over a green winter tree.
repair_sequence: Keep the evergreen green in every panel.
Ask learner to tap the tree that stays green.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner chooses evergreen from seasonal evidence.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use gentle garden sounds and no sudden movement.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use static picture cards and season panels.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read every prompt and plant name aloud with replay.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use picture cards and icons rather than text-heavy prompts.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Show three choices maximum in early tasks.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow repeated listening and card inspection.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Use discovery language: let's look again.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach plant, tree, flower, leaf, wild, garden and green.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires naming and sorting common plant groups; secure requires using simple observable features after spaced review.
evidence.parent_summary: Can spot common plants and trees and explain simple features such as staying green.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Identifies and sorts common wild plants, garden plants and trees using visual evidence.
next_actions: Connect plant naming to plant parts.
Use outdoor observation prompts with adult support.
Add retrieval cards to daily warm-up.

qa.notes: Sample Year 1 science pack designed for audio-first observation and sorting.
